lede: A look at February 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including George Harrison and Karl May, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Cuba
events.desc: Four people are killed and several more are injured when Cuban Border Guard Troops confront and open fire on a US-registered speedboat violating Cuban waters.

events.title: Baruch Goldstein
events.desc: American-Israeli extremist Baruch Goldstein commits a mass shooting at the Cave of the Patriarchs mausoleum, leaving 29 dead and over 100 injured before he is disarmed and beaten to death by survivors.

events.title: Suriname
events.desc: The government of Suriname is overthrown by a military coup led by Dési Bouterse.

events.title: On the Cult of Personality and Its Consequences
events.desc: In his speech On the Cult of Personality and Its Consequences, Nikita Khrushchev, leader of the Soviet Union, denounces Stalin.

events.title: USS Ranger (CV-4)
events.desc: Launch of the USS Ranger at Newport News, Virginia. It is the first purpose-built aircraft carrier to be commissioned by the US Navy.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler, having been stateless for seven years, obtains German citizenship when he is appointed a Brunswick state official by Dietrich Klagges, a fellow Nazi. As a result, Hitler is able to run for Reichspräsident in the 1932 election.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: In the Battle of Verdun, a German unit captures Fort Douaumont, keystone of the French defences, without a fight.

events.title: George Frideric Handel
events.desc: George Frideric Handel's opera Nero premieres in Hamburg.

events.title: Khosrow II
events.desc: Khosrow II, the last great Shah of the Sasanian Empire (Iran), is overthrown by his son Kavadh II.

events.title: AD 138
events.desc: Roman emperor Hadrian adopts Antoninus Pius as his son, effectively making him his successor.

births.name: George Harrison
births.line: English singer-songwriter, guitarist and film producer

births.name: George Reid
births.line: Scottish-Australian lawyer and politician, 4th Prime Minister of Australia

deaths.text: Dmitry Yazov, last Marshal of the Soviet Union
Glenn T. Seaborg, American chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Friedrich Spielhagen, German author, theorist, and translator
Paul Reuter, German-English journalist and businessman, founded Reuters
Otto Ludwig (writer), German author, playwright, and critic

observances.text: Christian feast days: Blessed Ciriaco María Sancha y Hervás
Christian feast days: John Roberts, writer and missionary (Anglican Communion)
Christian feast days: Hamburg Matthiae-mahl, feast of Hanseatic League cities on the mediaeval first day of spring
Christian feast days: Blessed Maria Adeodata Pisani
Christian feast days: Blessed Robert of Arbrissel, founder of Fontevraud Abbey
Christian feast days: Saint Walpurga (she was canonised on 1 May c. 870 and Walpurgis Night is celebrated 30 April)
